checks.a_analysis: The text in the output corresponds to the text found in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were identified.
No phone numbers, emails, or URLs appear to be fabricated. The document contains names and attendance notes, which match the source.
All pages (3) are present. While a large portion of section 10 is missing, the heading for it remains, classifying it under text omission rather than a missing section/page.

The tables for meeting attendees and voting records contain all the rows found in the source document. The attendee table is structured differently (merging several source tables into one side-by-side grid), but all data rows are present.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: Agenda item 10, 'Community College', is missing all its descriptive sub-paragraphs (a, b, and c). Only the main heading is present in the output. This represents a loss of several hundred words, which is 100% of the content for that specific section.
checks.b_evidence_source:

c) Be Rail Safe Training for Telecommunicators- Ms. Turbeville welcomed guests Roger Smock... [~75 words]

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The metadata title contains a factual error regarding the date of the meeting, stating 'April 21 2022' whereas the source document clearly states 'April 22, 2021' in the header.
checks.b_evidence_output: 911 Board Education Committee Meeting Minutes April 21 2022 685F9F9D

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated blocks of text. The content of the meeting minutes, attendee lists, and agenda items appear only once, corresponding to their single appearance in the source PDF.

The heading hierarchy follows a logical progression from H1 to H3 in the header section and uses H2 for the 'Agenda' section. There are no skipped levels or incorrect nestings that would hinder navigation for screen readers.

The attendee table on Page 1 (reading_order 7) is structurally corrupted. The pipeline attempts to merge side-by-side tables (Committee Members and Guests) into a single 6-column grid. However, for the 'Staff' section of the list (starting at row index 10), the output only provides 3 cells per row. In a 6-column table, this creates a structural inconsistency as the row length does not match the table definition established by the headers, making the table malformed.
checks.b_evidence_source: The Staff table on Page 1 is a separate 3-column table located below the Committee Members/Guests tables.
checks.b_evidence_output: The 'Staff' header row (index 10) and subsequent staff name rows in the table at reading_order 7 contain only 3 cells each, despite the table being established with 6 columns in the preceding rows.

checks.a_analysis: The reading order generally follows the logical flow of the document. The image (logo) is moved to the end of the first section, which is a common accessibility adjustment to prevent decorative elements from interrupting content flow, and does not impact the logical understanding of the minutes.

The output fails to correctly represent the list structures in the document. First, numbered lists (items 1-3 on page 1 and items 8-11 on page 3) are converted to unordered lists, and the numbers are stripped from the text. More critically, the nested sub-items for item 10 (a, b, and c) regarding 'Partnerships', 'Cape Fear Community College...', and 'Be Rail Safe...' are entirely omitted from the output. This results in both a loss of organizational structure and a significant loss of information.
checks.b_evidence_source: Item 10 on Page 3 contains sub-bullets a), b), and c) with extensive text descriptions.
checks.b_evidence_output: The list item for 'Community College' (reading_order 20, item index 2) contains only the header text and is immediately followed by item 11. The sub-items a, b, and c are missing.
checks.b_evidence_location: Page 3, Community College section, element index 20

checks.a_analysis: No links are present in the source document, and none have been incorrectly injected into the output.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The multi-column layout of the attendance tables on page 1 is successfully converted into a responsive table structure that preserves the relative positioning of Committee Members, Guests, and Staff. The sequence of agenda items 1 through 11 is correctly preserved in the flow of the document. Standard HTML differences like fonts and margins are present but do not affect the visual organization.

All tables from the source PDF are rendered correctly in the HTML. The complex attendance table on page 1, which features side-by-side tables in the original, is rendered as a single cohesive table structure that remains fully legible and understandable. The voting tables for agenda items 4 and 5 on page 2 are correctly rendered with distinct columns for 'Yes' and 'No' and clear row alignment. Headers are visually distinct.

The primary meaningful image from the source PDF, the 'NC DIT 911 Board' logo, is present in the HTML output. The 'Approved' watermark from the PDF is correctly omitted in the HTML as it is a decorative element and its removal is an accessibility best practice.

There is no visual duplication of body content (paragraphs, tables, or images) within the document. While headings appear in both the Table of Contents and the body, this is an expected accessibility feature and not a defect. The logo image appears once, and the header text appears once at the top of the body.
